title: भवानी अष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री भवानी अष्टकम् ॥

न तातो न माता न बन्धुर्न दाता
न पुत्रो न पुत्री न भृत्यो न भर्ता।
न जाया न विद्या न वृत्तिर्ममैव
गतिस्त्वं गतिस्त्वं त्वमेका भवानि॥१॥

भवाब्धावपारे महादुःखभीरु
पपात प्रकामी प्रलोभी प्रमत्तः।
कुसंसारपाशप्रबद्धः सदाहं
गतिस्त्वं गतिस्त्वं त्वमेका भवानि॥२॥

न जानामि दानं न च ध्यानयोगं
न जानामि तन्त्रं न च स्तोत्रमन्त्रम्।
न जानामि पूजां न च न्यासयोगं
गतिस्त्वं गतिस्त्वं त्वमेका भवानि॥३॥

न जानामि पुण्यं न जानामि तीर्थं
न जानामि मुक्तिं लयं वा कदाचित्।
न जानामि भक्तिं व्रतं वापि मातर्
गतिस्त्वं गतिस्त्वं त्वमेका भवानि॥४॥

कुकर्मी कुसंगी कुबुद्धिः कुदासः
कुलाचारहीनः कदाचारलीनः।
कुदृष्टिः कुवाक्यप्रबन्धः सदाहं
गतिस्त्वं गतिस्त्वं त्वमेका भवानि॥५॥

प्रजेशं रमेशं महेशं सुरेशं
दिनेशं निशीथेश्वरं वा कदाचित्।
न जानामि चान्यत्सदाहं शरण्ये
गतिस्त्वं गतिस्त्वं त्वमेका भवानि॥६॥

विवादे विषादे प्रमादे प्रवासे
जले चानले पर्वते शत्रुमध्ये।
अरण्ये शरण्ये सदा मां प्रपाहि
गतिस्त्वं गतिस्त्वं त्वमेका भवानि॥७॥

अनाथो दरिद्रो जरारोगयुक्तो
महाक्षीणदीनः सदा जाड्यवक्त्रः।
विपत्तौ प्रविष्टः प्रनष्टः सदाहं
गतिस्त्वं गतिस्त्वं त्वमेका भवानि॥८॥

॥ इति श्रीमदादिशंकराचार्यविरचितं भवानी अष्टकं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Bhavani Ashtakam ||

Na Tato Na Mata Na Bandhur Na Data
Na Putro Na Putri Na Bhrityo Na Bharta.
Na Jaya Na Vidya Na Vrittir Mamaiva
Gatis Tvam Gatis Tvam Tvam Eka Bhavani.||1||

Bhavabdhav Apare Maha-duhkha-bhiru
Papata Prakami Pralobhi Pramattah.
Ku-samsara-pasha-prabaddhah Sada-aham
Gatis Tvam Gatis Tvam Tvam Eka Bhavani.||2||

Na Janami Danam Na Cha Dhyana-yogam
Na Janami Tantram Na Cha Stotra-mantram.
Na Janami Pujam Na Cha Nyasa-yogam
Gatis Tvam Gatis Tvam Tvam Eka Bhavani.||3||

Na Janami Punyam Na Janami Tirtham
Na Janami Muktim Layam Va Kadachit.
Na Janami Bhaktim Vratam Vapi Matar
Gatis Tvam Gatis Tvam Tvam Eka Bhavani.||4||

Ku-karmi Ku-sangi Ku-buddhih Ku-dasah
Kulachara-hinah Kadachara-linah.
Ku-drishtih Ku-vakya-prabandhah Sada-aham
Gatis Tvam Gatis Tvam Tvam Eka Bhavani.||5||

Prajesham Ramesham Mahesham Suresham
Dinesham Nishitheshwaram Va Kadachit.
Na Janami Chanyat Sada-aham Sharanye
Gatis Tvam Gatis Tvam Tvam Eka Bhavani.||6||

Vivade Vishade Pramade Pravase
Jale Chanale Parvate Shatru-madhye.
Aranye Sharanye Sada Mam Prapahi
Gatis Tvam Gatis Tvam Tvam Eka Bhavani.||7||

Anatho Daridro Jara-roga-yukto
Maha-kshina-dinah Sada Jadya-vaktrah.
Vipattau Pravishtah Pranashtah Sada-aham
Gatis Tvam Gatis Tvam Tvam Eka Bhavani.||8||

|| Iti Shrimad Adi Shankaracharya-virachitam Bhavani Ashtakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
न पिता हैं, न माता, न बन्धु, न दाता, न पुत्र, न पुत्री, न सेवक, न पति, न पत्नी, न विद्या, न वृत्ति - मेरी गति केवल तुम्हीं हो, तुम्हीं हो, हे एकमात्र भवानी!

अपार भवसागर में महान दुःख से भयभीत, कामी, लोभी, प्रमत्त, संसार के पाश में सदा बँधा हुआ मैं - मेरी गति तुम्हीं हो, हे भवानी!

मुझे न दान आता है, न ध्यान-योग, न तन्त्र, न स्तोत्र-मन्त्र, न पूजा, न न्यास-योग। मुझे न पुण्य ज्ञात है, न तीर्थ, न मुक्ति, न भक्ति, न व्रत। मेरी गति तुम्हीं हो।

मैं कुकर्मी, कुसंगी, कुबुद्धि, बुरा दास हूँ। विवाद में, विषाद में, प्रमाद में, प्रवास में, जल में, अग्नि में, पर्वत पर, शत्रुओं के बीच, वन में - हे शरण्ये! सदा मेरी रक्षा करो।

मैं अनाथ, दरिद्र, वृद्धावस्था और रोग से युक्त, अत्यन्त क्षीण-दीन, विपत्ति में पड़ा, नष्ट हो रहा हूँ - मेरी गति तुम्हीं हो, हे एकमात्र भवानी!
benefits: One of the most heartfelt prayers of complete surrender to the Divine Mother
Invokes divine refuge to those who feel helpless and without support
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of fears, sorrows, and feelings of abandonment
Invokes the blessing of the unconditional grace of goddess bhavani
Ideal for those who feel they have no other spiritual recourse
Composed by Adi Shankaracharya as supreme devotional surrender
description: Dedicated to Goddess Durga, the Bhavani Ashtakam (भवानी अष्टकम्) is an eight-verse devotional hymn revered in Hindu tradition. Drawn from Composed by Adi Shankaracharya, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to grant divine refuge to those who feel helpless and without support and composed by Adi Shankaracharya as supreme devotional surrender. It is also said to remove all fears, sorrows, and feelings of abandonment. This sacred text is ideally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar), Friday (Shukravar), and Monday (Somvar) and during Navratri for enhanced blessings. With 8 verses and a reading time of about 5 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Bhavani Ashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Bhavani Ashtakam by Shankaracharya in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Eight verses of complete surrender to Goddess Bhavani for divine refuge.

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Bhavani Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Bhavani Ashtakam is traditionally attributed to Adi Shankaracharya, the eighth century Advaita acharya whose stotras span every major deity of the Hindu pantheon. The text is a deeply emotional surrender hymn in which Shankara, the supreme philosopher of non-dualism, casts off all intellectual self-sufficiency and declares that he has neither father nor mother, neither son nor daughter, neither friend nor relation, neither knowledge nor wealth — Bhavani alone is his sole refuge. The recurring refrain Gatistvam Gatistvam Tvameka Bhavani (You alone are my refuge, O Bhavani) appears at the end of each verse, making it one of the most moving sharanagati hymns in the Sanskrit corpus. (Sources: Hindupedia Shankaracharya; Stotra Nidhi; Wikipedia Adi Shankara.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Bhavani in this stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: Bhavani literally means the consort of Bhava (Shiva) and is one of the oldest names of the Great Goddess. She is identified with Parvati in her benevolent maternal form, with Durga as the slayer of Mahishasura, and with the local kuldevata of many Maharashtrian, Karnataka and Gujarat clans. The Tulja Bhavani temple at Tuljapur in Maharashtra is the most famous Bhavani shrine; she is the family deity of the Bhonsle dynasty, and Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj is said to have received the celebrated Bhavani sword and the inspiration to found Hindavi Swarajya from her grace. The Ashtakam invokes this same Bhavani as Jagat-janani, the World-Mother. (Sources: Wikipedia Tulja Bhavani; Hindupedia Bhavani; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Na Tato Na Mata in the Bhavani Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening line Na Tato Na Mata Na Bandhur Na Data, Na Putro Na Putri Na Bhrityo Na Bharta translates as Neither father nor mother, neither relation nor benefactor, neither son nor daughter, neither servant nor husband (do I have). It is a complete renunciation of every worldly relationship and identity that ordinarily anchors human security. The verse continues Na Jaya Na Vidya Na Vrittirmamaiva, Gatistvam Gatistvam Tvameka Bhavani — Neither wife, nor knowledge, nor occupation is mine; You alone, O Bhavani, are my refuge. This radical surrender posture distinguishes the ashtakam from ordinary praise hymns and makes it a quintessential text of bhakti as absolute helplessness before the Mother. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia; Sanskritdocuments.org.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite Bhavani Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: Tuesday and Friday are the traditional weekdays of Devi worship; Monday is also recommended in the Shaiva-Shakta tradition since Bhavani is the consort of Shiva. Sharad Navaratri in Ashvin and Chaitra Navaratri in Chaitra are the supreme annual periods, with daily recitation across all nine nights. Other auspicious occasions include Sankranti, Purnima especially Sharad Purnima, Magha Gupt Navratri, Ashada Gupt Navratri, and the annual Tulja Bhavani Yatra at Tuljapur. The stotra is also commonly recited in moments of personal crisis, illness, bereavement and despair as the verses are designed precisely for surrender at such moments. (Sources: Drikpanchang Navratri; Hindupedia Tulja Bhavani; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Bhavani Ashtakam confer?
seoFaqs.answer: Although the ashtakam itself does not contain a conventional phalashruti listing material rewards, the traditional phala associated with daily recitation includes profound mental peace through the cultivation of surrender, removal of fear and anxiety especially in periods of upheaval, dissolution of the ahankara that resists divine grace, protection from sudden calamity, and the steady ripening of bhakti into jnana. Devotees in distress have testified across centuries to its capacity to transform helplessness into refuge. The non-transactional nature of the prayer — one asks for nothing but Bhavani herself as refuge — is precisely what classical commentators identify as its highest reward. (Sources: Hindupedia; Sanatan Sanstha; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: How long is the Bhavani Ashtakam and how should it be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ashtakam contains eight verses, each ending with the refrain Gatistvam Gatistvam Tvameka Bhavani, and takes about four to six minutes at devotional pace. The traditional method is to sit facing east or north on a clean asana, light a lamp before an image of Bhavani or Durga, recite a brief Ganesha prayer, then chant the eight verses with full attention to the meaning of each renunciation phrase. Many devotees follow it with Bhavani's bija mantra or her ashtottara shatanamavali. The stotra works best when recited slowly enough to actually feel the weight of each negation rather than mechanically. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Sanatan Sanstha; Hindupedia.)
